html:
Schweiter is not the old “cash equals market cap” stub. The corrected setup is better described as a neglected Swiss specialty-composites recovery at about CHF 278 per share, roughly CHF 398 million market cap, 1.43 million shares, and CHF 56 million official net liquidity. The stock is cheap because 2025 reported EPS was negative, but that loss was driven by the Bus & Rail exit, not by a broken core business.

The forward story is a cleaner 3A Composites portfolio emerging from a cyclical trough. In 2025, sales fell to CHF 904 million, adjusted EBITDA was CHF 74 million, free operating cash flow was CHF 39 million, and the company still paid a CHF 15 dividend, now a roughly 5.4% yield. The real valuation is about 4.5-5.2x adjusted EBITDA and 8-10x trough free cash flow, depending on lease treatment. On normalized economics, it is cheaper: management’s cycle ambition is 7-9% EBIT margin, which on CHF 900-950 million of sales implies CHF 63-86 million of EBIT versus CHF 34 million adjusted in 2025.

The market label is “European building-products small cap with negative earnings.” The variant label is “portfolio-cleaned lightweight-composites platform with data-center facade exposure, China wind kitting, recycled-PET cost advantages, and self-help margin recovery.” Schweiter’s 2025 release shows Bus & Rail was divested, the Accelerate cost program is complete, Core Materials remained above group profitability, Architecture gained European share, and North American facade sales were helped by data centers and commercial projects. Management also said 2026 net income should be clearly positive because the CHF 27.5 million accounting hit and CHF 3.5 million operating loss from Mobility should not recur.

The torque is not financial leverage. The balance sheet is conservative. The torque is operating leverage from a depressed denominator. If Schweiter only gets back to 10% EBITDA margin, management’s first intermediate milestone, EBITDA rises from CHF 74 million to roughly CHF 90 million on flat sales. If the full cycle target is approached, EBIT can nearly double. At that point, current market cap is only 6-8x normalized earnings and roughly 5-6x normalized free cash flow.

There are three underappreciated demand pockets. First, U.S. data-center construction is a real vertical for ALUCOBOND facades. Management estimated a CHF 50 million U.S. data-center facade market with about 10% current share and ambition to grow it; CBRE’s 2026 outlook says U.S. data-center leasing is on track for another record year with preleasing still around the mid-70% range. Second, Core Materials is not just generic wind exposure. Schweiter has balsa plus PET, expanded kitting in China, and claims it is gaining Chinese share through total-cost advantages such as lower resin uptake. GWEC’s 2026 report shows global wind additions jumped to 165 GW in 2025, with China adding more than 120 GW. Third, European construction is turning from trough to recovery. Euroconstruct’s winter 2025 work points to 2.4% growth for 2026 across its 19 markets after only 0.3% growth in 2025.

Capital allocation is the main irritant. A shareholder directly challenged management on why there is no buyback at a more than 10% free-cash-flow yield. Management said the dividend has priority and buybacks are not currently the focus. That is not optimal. But it also creates a possible catalyst: the stock is illiquid, under-owned, down about 30% over one year, and any shift from dividend-only to even a modest buyback would be highly accretive. For now, the CHF 15 dividend pays investors to wait while operating performance recovers.

Bear case: CHF 230, -17% from CHF 278. Display demand stays weak, China wind remains price-competitive, Architecture’s data-center opportunity is too small to offset soft construction, and management keeps returning only dividends while margins stagnate around 8% EBITDA. The stock trades around 0.5x book and 7x trough EBITDA.

Base case: CHF 440, +58%. Sales stabilize around CHF 900-930 million, Mobility losses disappear, Core Materials improves in H2 2026, and procurement plus Accelerate savings lift EBITDA to CHF 90-95 million. Apply 6x EBITDA plus net liquidity, or about 12x CHF 30-32 of normalized EPS, and the stock is worth roughly CHF 430-450.

Bull case: CHF 650, +134%. Data-center facades, European construction recovery, recycled-PET products, and China wind kitting push sales back toward CHF 950 million while EBIT margin reaches the lower end of the 7-9% cycle target. That means CHF 65-75 million EBIT, CHF 45-55 million net income, and CHF 32-38 EPS, with CHF 60-70 million free cash flow. At 14-15x earnings or 7x EBITDA, plus a still strong balance sheet, CHF 650 is defensible.
